title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
Dr. Arthur Calgary visits the Agar family to deliver news regarding Jack Agar, who was convicted of murder and subsequently died in prison. Calgary presents himself as a reputable witness, explaining that medical evidence and specific details from Dulmace corroborate Jack's innocence. He informs the family that justice can be sought through a pardon from the Queen, which would allow the newspapers to report the facts and clear Jack's reputation.

The revelation causes significant distress among the family members. Gwendraith Vane eventually urges Calgary to leave so the family can process the shock. As Calgary prepares to depart, Miss Corston Lindström confronts him, criticizing him for forcing the family to relive their trauma and arguing that Jack was a bad man regardless of his innocence. At the door, Hester Agar reproaches Calgary, questioning the utility of seeking justice for a dead man and stating that the news only harms the living.

After Calgary leaves for Draymour in a taxi, Hester retreats inside. In the sitting room, Miss Lindström offers Hester brandy, and the two discuss the suddenness of the news. While Hester acknowledges Calgary's courage in delivering the information, Miss Lindström remains skeptical, suggesting there may be possibilities Calgary has overlooked. Meanwhile, in the study, Leo Agar attempts to contact Mary and Mike Darrington. Leo informs them that Dr. Calgary brought a letter from the defense lawyer, Andrew Marshall, which reveals that Jacqueline's account regarding her whereabouts on the night of the murder was true.
